MemberSignature.Value: public System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList<Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceMachineIPAddress> IPAddresses { get; }
.property instance class System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList`1<class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceMachineIPAddress> IPAddresses

member this.IPAddresses : System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList<Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceMachineIPAddress>

Docs.summary: IPv4, IPv6 addresses of the machine.

MemberSignature.Value: public bool? IsNodePublicIpEnabled { get; }
.property instance valuetype System.Nullable`1<bool> IsNodePublicIpEnabled
Public ReadOnly Property IsNodePublicIpEnabled As Nullable(Of Boolean)
member this.IsNodePublicIpEnabled : Nullable<bool>

Docs.summary: Whether the machine is allocated its own public IP. Some scenarios may require the machine to receive their own dedicated public IP addresses. A common scenario is for gaming workloads, where a console needs to make a direct connection to a cloud virtual machine to minimize hops. The default is false.

MemberSignature.Value: public Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier NodePublicIpPrefixId { get; }
.property instance class Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier NodePublicIpPrefixId
Public ReadOnly Property NodePublicIpPrefixId As ResourceIdentifier
member this.NodePublicIpPrefixId : Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier

Docs.summary: The public IP prefix ID which VM node should use IPs from. This is of the form: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/publicIPPrefixes/{publicIPPrefixName}.

MemberSignature.Value: public Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier PodSubnetId { get; }
.property instance class Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier PodSubnetId
Public ReadOnly Property PodSubnetId As ResourceIdentifier
member this.PodSubnetId : Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier

Docs.summary: The ID of the subnet which pods will join when launched. If omitted, pod IPs are statically assigned on the node subnet (see vnetSubnetID for more details). This is of the form: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkName}/subnets/{subnetName}.

MemberSignature.Value: public Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier VnetSubnetId { get; }
.property instance class Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier VnetSubnetId
Public ReadOnly Property VnetSubnetId As ResourceIdentifier
member this.VnetSubnetId : Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier

Docs.summary: The ID of the subnet which node and optionally pods will join on startup. If this is not specified, a VNET and subnet will be generated and used. If no podSubnetID is specified, this applies to nodes and pods, otherwise it applies to just nodes. This is of the form: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkName}/subnets/{subnetName}.
